tools.name: get_fabrication_quote
tools.description: Get ranked, purchasable offers (price, ETA, preview image) for fabricating a physical item from a design file. process=fdm_print for 3D printing a model (STL/OBJ/PLY/3MF/AMF/STEP/IGES), process=cnc or process=sheetmetal for machined/bent metal parts (STEP, IGES, DXF), process=decal for stickers/deca

tools.name: refine_quote
tools.description: Narrow an existing quote with new constraints (destination ZIP the user just provided, deadline, outdoor/waterproof/heat/food-safety requirements, budget, vendor reliability, verbatim requirements terms, drawing callouts, or a process filter — "no, I meant in sheet metal" → processes) — or CHANGE it

tools.name: create_checkout
tools.description: Create the order and get a hosted payment link for a chosen offer. Call this as soon as the user picks an offer + shipping speed — do NOT ask for their address first: the secure payment page collects the shipping address and email. Only pass address fields if the user already volunteered them. Retur

tools.name: get_order_status
tools.description: Check status, ETA and tracking for a UFP order.

tools.name: list_orders
tools.description: List this agent's recent UFP orders (newest first): order id, what was made, vendor, status, total, ETA, created date. Use it to resolve vague references — "my sticker order", "that order from 6 days ago" — to a concrete order_id before calling get_order_status or leave_review.

tools.name: leave_review
tools.description: Record the user's verified-purchase review of how a UFP order turned out. YOUR JOB IS TO STRUCTURE THE FEEDBACK: turn what the user actually said into stars (1-5 overall) and facet scores — "the cut was sloppy" -> quality, "parts arrived 2 days late" -> timeliness, "wrong color" -> accuracy. Keep th

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "list_orders" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: List this agent's recent UFP orders (newest first): order id, what was made, vendor, status, total, ETA, created date. Use it to resolve vague references — "my …

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "leave_review" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Record the user's verified-purchase review of how a UFP order turned out. YOUR JOB IS TO STRUCTURE THE FEEDBACK: turn what the user actually said into stars (1-…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_fabrication_quote, create_checkout · Private data: get_fabrication_quote, leave_review · Public sink: get_fabrication_quote, create_checkout, leave_review.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_fabrication_quote, create_checkout · Private data: get_fabrication_quote, leave_review · Public sink: get_fabrication_quote, create_checkout, leave_review

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
